description: A coverage skeptic who bypasses the UI and reads docs/data.json directly. Demands that every active event_category the site advertises is populated with at least 3 events, so no category is a Potemkin label.

asserts.expression: try { var raw=document.body.innerText; var events=JSON.parse(raw); if(!Array.isArray(events)) return false; var counts={}; events.forEach(function(e){var c=e.event_category||e.type||'unknown'; counts[c]=(counts[c]||0)+1;}); window.__categoryCounts=counts; var active=Object.keys(counts).filter(function(k){return k!=='unknown' && k!=='other';}); var underfilled=active.filter(function(k){return counts[k] < 3;}); window.__underfilled=underfilled; return underfilled.length===0 && active.length>=4; } catch(e){ window.__catErr=String(e); return false; }
try { var events=JSON.parse(document.body.innerText); return events.length >= 50; } catch(e){ return false; }

llm.system_prompt: You are the comprehensiveness-user. Your goals: verify the calendar covers enough Austin venues in my preferred category. Critique this Austin cultural events calendar site through your lens. Identify anything blocking your primary user story in <=150 words. Start with PASS or FAIL.
llm.goals: verify the calendar covers enough Austin venues in my preferred category
